title: arthropteris submarginalis
intro: Arthropteris submarginalis is a species of fern that belongs to the family Cyatheaceae, commonly found in New Zealand. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, where it is believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Arthropteris submarginalis are thought to provide health benefits such as relieving pain, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
